dsl_rule.translation_notes: Signal describes breakout with pullback monitoring. Entry uses Donchian channel crosses to identify breakouts in both directions. Exit conditions use pivot support (s1) and resistance (r1) levels as 'nearby support zones' mentioned by author. Hold period set to 14 days (average pullback timeframe stated). Stop loss set conservatively at 2% given author's silence on exact placement. Pattern performs best near yearly extremes per author's regime analysis, but this cannot be directly encoded with allowed indicators—recommend external regime confirmation. Success rate metrics unavailable (AUTHOR_SILENT), so no statistical filters applied.

source_rule_text: Breakout pullback behavior: A pullback occurs about half the time after breakout, taking on average nearly two weeks to return to breakout price. Performance suffers when pullbacks occur.
